steps.description: Click on the "Search" input field in the "Samples" tab within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.

Type text: 'foundry' into the search bar located at the top of the "Samples" pane within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface. This initiates a search to filter sample projects matching the keyword.
Click the "Create" button next to the "Microsoft Foundry Agent" sample in the "Featured samples" section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click the "Default folder" text input field in the "Workspace Folder" dialog box to set the project directory at `/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects`.
Click the "File" menu in the top-left corner of the Visual Studio Code interface to open its drop-down menu.
Click the "Open Workspace from File..." option in the "File" dropdown menu in the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click on the file named "ProxyAgent-CSharp.code-workspace" with the type "Visual Studio Code - Insiders Workspace" in the file selection dialog at coordinates (406, 129).

Click the "Open" button located in the bottom-right corner of the file selection dialog to confirm the selection of "ProxyAgent-CSharp.code-workspace" in the context of choosing a workspace file for Visual Studio Code.

parameters.sample:

```bash
mkdir -p /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/ProxyAgent-CSharp/M365Agent/env
```

steps.description: @code to create a folder named 'env' in "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/ProxyAgent-CSharp/M365Agent/",then create a '.env.local' file in "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/ProxyAgent-CSharp/M365Agent/env"

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: bash

```bash
#!/bin/bash

set -e

# Define the file path
file_path="/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/ProxyAgent-CSharp/M365Agent/env/.env.local"

# Check if the file exists
if [[ ! -f "$file_path" ]]; then
  echo "File does not exist: $file_path" >&2
  exit 1
fi

# Define the new entries
newline1="AZURE_AI_FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ENDPOINT=${{secret:AZURE_AI_FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ENDPOINT:}}"
newline2="AGENT_ID=${{secret:AGENT_ID:}}"

# Append the new items to the file
echo -e "$newline1\n$newline2" >> "$file_path"

echo "New items added successfully."
```

=== Execution Results ===
Exit Code: 0

=== STDOUT ===
New items added successfully.

✓ Script executed successfully

steps.description: @code  add new line and input new item AZURE_AI_FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ENDPOINT=${{secret:AZURE_AI_FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ENDPOINT}}, then press enter to start a new line and input a new item AGENT_ID=${{secret:AGENT_ID}} for the file "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/ProxyAgent-CSharp/M365Agent/env/.env.local"

@code press enter to start a new line and input new item `SSO_APP_ID=` to the file "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/ProxyAgent-CSharp/M365Agent/env/.env.local", then press enter to start a new line and input a new item `BOT_DOMAIN=`, then press enter to start a new line and input a new item `BOT_ENDPOINT=`,then press enter to start a new line and input a new item `TUNNEL_ID=`,
@code change file /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/ProxyAgent-CSharp/M365Agent/scripts/devtunnel.sh as executable file
@code run script `devtunnel user login`
Click the "${{env:AZURE_ACCOUNT_NAME}}" account option on the Microsoft sign-in selection screen within Google Chrome.
Type text: '${{secret:AZURE_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}}' in the password input field on the Microsoft login page for user '${{env:AZURE_ACCOUNT_NAME}} '.
Press the "Enter" key to submit the password and trigger the "Sign in" button on the Microsoft login screen visible within a web browser.
Click the browser's close ("X") button located at the top-right corner of the tab to close it and end the session.

Press the "Enter" key to confirm the selection of a resource group from the dropdown in the "Select a resource group" dialog within Visual Studio Code, specifically in the Microsoft 365 Agents extension interface.
Press 'Enter' to confirm the input of the "New resource group name" field in the Visual Studio Code extension for Microsoft 365 Agents.
Press "Enter" to confirm the selection of the "Central US" region in the dropdown list within the "Loading options – new resource group" dialog box.
Click the "Provision" button in the confirmation dialog prompting Azure account provisioning within the Visual Studio Code interface.
